description_long: This is a two-story pagoda structure with a vibrant red exterior and a prominent green-tiled, multi-tiered roof. The upper level features a wraparound balcony with wooden railings, while the lower level is adorned with lush potted plants and climbing vines, adding a touch of nature to the building. The roof tiles are arranged in a traditional overlapping pattern, and the eaves are slightly upturned, characteristic of East Asian architecture. There are open windows and a small seating area with stools on one side, suggesting a welcoming, communal space. Decorative elements such as hanging plants and a fabric banner add to the building's charm. The structure sits on a paved base, indicating its placement in an urban or garden setting.
description: A two-story red pagoda with green-tiled, upturned roofs, wooden balconies, and lush plant decorations, set on a paved base.
description_view_0: Front view showing the main entrance, potted plants, and the lower roof with a balcony above.
description_view_1: Rear view highlighting dense greenery climbing the back wall and a visible fabric banner on the upper level.
description_view_2: Side view displaying the layered roof structure, balcony railings, and additional greenery.
description_view_3: Side view with open windows, two wooden stools, and a clear view of the lower level's seating area.
